config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan mengorbankannya seenaknya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika individu dilindungi, lalu diajak berkontribusi secara adil untuk kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should always take priority over the individual, because in my view that can easily become a justification for suppressing ordinary people. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize social stability, collective responsibility, and the common good, especially after seeing how disorder can hurt everyone, but that does not mean individual rights should be treated as secondary by default. In practice, when the “interest of society” is defined too broadly, it can be used to silence dissent, limit personal freedom, and excuse unfair treatment. I believe the better principle is that society and individuals should be balanced, with basic rights protected first and collective interests pursued only within clear limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia kita terlalu sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa disisihkan seenaknya, yang terjadi bukan solidaritas melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi, lalu diminta berkontribusi secara adil dan sukarela, bukan dipaksa tunduk pada tafsir kepentingan sosial yang kabur.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability and collective order, but I have also seen how easily “the greater good” can become a vague excuse for silencing ordinary people or ignoring unfair treatment. A society is not truly healthy if it demands that basic rights be sacrificed whenever authorities claim it is necessary. The better approach is to protect individual rights as the baseline, then pursue social goals within clear and limited boundaries.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia frasa “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai tameng untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling lemah. Stabilitas dan ketertiban memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar individu bisa disingkirkan begitu saja, yang lahir bukan kebaikan bersama melainkan kekuasaan yang sulit dikoreksi. Menurut saya, kepentingan sosial harus dibatasi oleh hak individu, bukan sebaliknya. Kalau masyarakat ingin kuat, caranya bukan menekan warga, melainkan melindungi mereka lalu membangun tanggung jawab bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in my view that principle too easily turns into a blank check for abuse. From my experience and cultural background in China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective responsibility, but I do not accept the idea that those goals should override basic rights by default. When “the public interest” is defined too broadly, it can hide unfairness and silence people who have no power. A strong society should protect individuals first, then ask them to contribute to the common good within clear limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
